BLEU Boston, MA
BLEU› Anxiety› Boston, MA
◈ Anxiety◐ Sleep◉ Gut Health

Harvard, MIT, Tufts, BU, BC, Northeastern — and that's just a few. Boston is the most educated city in the country and one of its most quietly anxious.

Chronic cortisol elevation has locked your nervous system in a state it cannot exit without targeted intervention.

Local data — Boston, MA Boston college students report anxiety at 2x the national average. The culture spreads beyond campus.
The street truth

Achievement culture doesn't stay on campus. It spreads into how everyone in this city relates to work, to success, to being enough. The winters are cold and the standards are higher and the pressure is historical. Your nervous system picked all of it up.

What it feels like
  • Feeling on edge even when everything is technically fine
  • Overthinking every conversation, decision, interaction
  • Chest tightness, shallow breathing, racing heart
  • Using something — alcohol, food, scrolling — to come down at night
Root cause analysis
  • Elevated baseline cortisol — the system is always braced for impact
  • HPA axis dysregulation — the stress response no longer self-corrects
  • Neurotransmitter imbalance — GABA suppressed, glutamate elevated
Primary driver in Boston
Academic pressure + New England cold + high-achieving culture = chronic anxiety baseline elevated.
Across Boston
From Beacon Hill to Dorchester, from Cambridge to Somerville — the pressure to achieve is constant.
The honest ask

You're not failing to manage stress. Your stress management system is overwhelmed. There's a difference, and there's a protocol for the second one.

Start Anxiety Regulation Protocol → Dr. Felicia Stoler · DCN MS RDN FACSM · Jazz Bird 501(c)(3)

☀️
Morning light — 10 minutes outside
Anchors cortisol curve. First and most important step.
7:15 AM
🌿
Take Ashwagandha KSM-66 600mg
With food. Builds over 2–4 weeks — consistency matters.
12:00 PM
🫁
Box breathing — 4 rounds
Interrupt the midday cortisol spike before it lands
3:00 PM
☕
No caffeine after this point
Half-life of caffeine is 6–8 hours. It's still active at 11pm.
5:00 PM
💊
Take Magnesium L-Threonate 2g
Crosses blood-brain barrier. Evening calm begins.
9:00 PM
🎧
Play Alpha Wave Track
8–12 Hz · 45 min · Entrains calm alertness before sleep

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) Research Foundation

Every protocol on BLEU is grounded in peer-reviewed research. The following studies inform the Anxiety Regulation Protocol. Jazz Bird's causal research pipeline will produce publishable findings from BLEU's outcome data — expanding this evidence base in real time.

HPA Axis Dysregulation and Anxiety
Chronic stress produces lasting changes to HPA axis sensitivity, resulting in elevated baseline cortisol that persists even in the absence of acute stressors.
Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 2015
Ashwagandha and Cortisol
KSM-66 ashwagandha (600mg/day) reduced anxiety scores by 69% and serum cortisol by 27.9% in a randomized controlled trial over 60 days.
Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine, 2012
Magnesium and Anxiety
Magnesium deficiency is associated with heightened anxiety responses. Supplementation shows anxiolytic effects comparable to diazepam in animal models.
Neuropharmacology, 2012
Alpha Wave Activity and Anxiety Reduction
Neurofeedback training to increase alpha wave activity (8–12 Hz) produced significant reductions in trait anxiety scores maintained at 3-month follow-up.
Applied Psychophysiology and Biofeedback, 2008
Vagus Nerve and Stress Response
Vagal tone predicts stress reactivity. Slow paced breathing activates vagal pathways and reduces cortisol within 5 minutes.
Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 2018
The Research Mission

Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) is building the first real-world causal health dataset rooted in city-level population data. Every check-in from every user in Boston becomes an anonymized data point. When patterns emerge — when BLEU sees that a specific intervention works specifically in Boston — that becomes a publishable finding. That finding becomes a grant. That grant funds more research. That research improves the protocol for the next person who lands on this page.

This is why Jazz Bird is a 501(c)(3). The research belongs to the community it serves.

Your journal trains your personal protocol. Every entry is a data point. Every data point makes the next recommendation more precise. This is how BLEU gets better for you specifically — not for everyone in general.

Every entry you make in Boston becomes an anonymized data point in Jazz Bird's population dataset. When enough people in Boston log similar patterns, the system detects it — and the protocol improves for everyone here.
BLEU · ALVAI · Boston, MA

ALVAI knows your city. It knows the signal — the academic and medical intensity. It knows the condition. Ask it anything about your anxiety protocol. It cross-references 7 federal health databases, 302,000 drug interaction pairs, and Jazz Bird's city-level intelligence in real time.

◐
ALVAI active for Boston, MA — Anxiety protocol. I know the academic and medical intensity. I know what that does to the body. Ask me anything.
Common questions from Boston
ALVAI provides information, not medical advice. Drug interactions are flagged for review, not prescribed. Dr. Felicia Stoler audits the knowledge infrastructure.

The Hand-Holding Network
Nobody in Boston knows anyone else using BLEU. Their data never touches. But when 438 people in Boston report that week 2 is the hardest — the system tells the next person before they get there. That's the hand-holding network. Invisible. Compounding. Yours.
